title: CKD follow-up

examination.text: Supports careful medical, family, medicine, urinary, systemic, examination, urinalysis, ACR, electrolyte, and imaging context in clinician-led CKD evaluation.
examination.rationale: Supports evaluation of cause and kidney-function context for chronic kidney disease follow-up; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports careful medical, family, medicine, urinary, systemic, examination, urinalysis, ACR, electrolyte, and imaging context in clinician-led CKD evaluation.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease

exact_section.heading: Evaluation of cause and kidney-function context
exact_section.locator: Practice Point 1.1.4.1, Figure 8, Table 6, and Practice Point 1.2.1.1; summary PDF pages 34–35 and 61

investigations.text: Supports GFR and urine-albumin testing, repeat confirmation, prior GFR or albuminuria trends, imaging, history, and the minimum chronicity context without diagnosing CKD from one result.
investigations.rationale: Supports CKD detection and chronicity review for chronic kidney disease follow-up; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports GFR and urine-albumin testing, repeat confirmation, prior GFR or albuminuria trends, imaging, history, and the minimum chronicity context without diagnosing CKD from one result.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease

exact_section.heading: Detection of CKD and evaluation of chronicity
exact_section.locator: Practice Points 1.1.1.1–1.1.1.2 and 1.1.3.1–1.1.3.2; summary PDF pages 34 and 54–57

medication.text: Supports periodic medication review, accurate medication lists, adherence, continued indication, interaction, monitoring, reconciliation, and nephrotoxic-medicine context without treatment changes or doses.
medication.rationale: Supports medicine stewardship for chronic kidney disease follow-up; no decision is generated.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports periodic medication review, accurate medication lists, adherence, continued indication, interaction, monitoring, reconciliation, and nephrotoxic-medicine context without treatment changes or doses.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease

exact_section.heading: Polypharmacy and drug stewardship
exact_section.locator: Practice Point 4.3.1 and Figure 46; summary PDF pages 50 and 135–137

referral.text: Supports clinician-led referral documentation for qualified sustained GFR decline, significant albuminuria with haematuria, refractory hypertension, persistent potassium abnormalities, and other listed circumstances.
referral.rationale: Supports qualified specialist kidney-care referral context for chronic kidney disease follow-up; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led referral documentation for qualified sustained GFR decline, significant albuminuria with haematuria, refractory hypertension, persistent potassium abnormalities, and other listed circumstances.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease

exact_section.heading: Referral to specialist kidney care services
exact_section.locator: Practice Point 5.1.1 and Figure 48; summary PDF pages 51 and 140–141

follow_up.text: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.
follow_up.rationale: Supports GFR and albuminuria monitoring for chronic kidney disease follow-up; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease

exact_section.heading: Monitoring GFR and albuminuria in CKD
exact_section.locator: Practice Points 2.1.1–2.1.5; summary PDF pages 40 and 81–82

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Cause, stage, GFR and ACR trends, blood pressure, complications, symptoms, medicines, adherence, examinations, tests, diagnosis, treatment and follow-up remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for chronic kidney disease follow-up.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
